_comment: Canonical Claude Code hooks block for agent VMs. Single source of truth: base/09 composes ~/.claude/settings.json from it at provision time, and the-assistant docs/agents/scripts/agent-redeploy.sh re-merges it onto existing boxes (base/09 is not re-run there). Commands are literal — $HOME/$PORTAL_URL/$AGENT_TOKEN/$AGENT_NAME expand when Claude runs the hook. The last-tool-use writer, the tool-event forwarder, the stop hook, and the needs-input request forwarder (all installed by base/14) each gate themselves to the JOB session by comparing their stdin session_id against job-context.json so lingering/operator sessions cannot pollute job liveness or reporting (JOB-SIGNAL-ATTRIBUTION plan). The .* tool-event forwarder posts a jq-REDUCED payload (byte-derived token estimates, never raw tool_input/tool_response) to /api/agents/events for per-MCP-server attribution (SCRUM-512); it skips TaskCreate/TaskUpdate, which the dedicated entry posts in full. sb-post-request.sh (SCRUM-1373) makes a BLOCKED agent visible in the portal: PreToolUse AskUserQuestion|ExitPlanMode opens a needs-input request, the Notification hook opens permission/idle requests, and PostToolUse AskUserQuestion|ExitPlanMode + the Stop hook resolve them — POSTed reduced + backgrounded + silent to /api/agents/requests. sb-await-decision.sh (SCRUM-1375) is the RETURN half: the second PreToolUse AskUserQuestion|ExitPlanMode command long-polls GET /api/agents/requests/:key for the operator's answer (recorded via POST /api/agents/requests/:id/resolve, SCRUM-1374) and maps it to a Claude Code permission allow/deny (+reason) so a portal pick unblocks the run without the Live desktop. It is strictly gated to the job session (blocks ONLY when stdin session_id == job-context session_id) and exits 0 with no output on any timeout/error/desktop-resolve, so free-form/idle/timeout still fall through to the desktop and a non-job session is never blocked. sb-drain-steer.sh (SCRUM-1378) is the agent-side CONSUMER of the operator steer queue (the missing half of SCRUM-1376): a 3rd PostToolUse `.*` command that, throttled to once per ~12s, drains GET /api/agents/steer scoped to its live session_id and feeds the operator's free-form hint(s) to the running Claude as hookSpecificOutput.additionalContext (then acks them). Like sb-await-decision it rides Claude Code's native hook-output channel — no tmux/VNC keystroke injection — and exits 0 with NO stdout on any miss so a tool call is never disturbed. sb-session-start.sh (SCRUM-1394, installed by base/14) is the SessionStart baseline writer: it snapshots each workspace repo's HEAD into ~/.sidebutton/session-heads-<session_id>.json so the Stop hook's git capture scopes to the repos THIS session actually advanced (and derives a real sha_start instead of a collapsed range); gated to the job session like the others, best-effort, and capture falls back to legacy behavior when no baseline exists. sb-clear-session-stopped.sh (SCRUM-1769, installed by base/14) is the UserPromptSubmit half of session-tidy: on hook_event_name=Stop ONLY (never SubagentStop, which fires mid-run) the stop hook marks the finished session — ~/.sidebutton/session-stopped/<session_id>.json = {pid, pid_start, stopped_at} — and the sb-session-tidy timer (base/19e, every 5min) closes that TUI once the mark is older than SB_SESSION_CLOSE_TTL_SEC (default 3600 = 60min; 0 disables), reclaiming the RAM that otherwise accumulates 12-23 sessions per box per day until the box OOM-livelocks. This hook removes the mark the instant the session is re-engaged, so a session working on a new prompt is never closed, and the next Stop rewrites it so the clock always runs from the LAST completion. The mark is written BEFORE the stop hook's job-session gate (lingering + operator sessions are exactly the ones that pile up) and is strictly best-effort, so it can never abort the POSTs below; it is self-scoped to the prompt's own session_id here, so no job-context gate is needed. Job completeness is still signalled ONLY by the Stop hook's step-complete POST: session-tidy is hard-decoupled from completion — it makes no portal calls, reads no job state, and uses no idle heuristic (the sentinel is written once at the genuine end of a run, immune to the transcript/CPU activity that defeated the retired reapers), so it neither is nor feeds a completion signal.
